title: Register a brand and give it a voice
headline: Tell the engine who it is writing as — then either feed it your content or start cold.
body: A "brand" is the account the engine writes for. Register at least one, then give it a Brand DNA:
the voice, the rules, the facts it must never get wrong.
Two honest paths, both fully supported:
• Have existing content? Point the engine at your files/exports, or auto-fetch from your public
account, and it will analyze your real voice and draft the DNA for you.
• Starting fresh? Use the cold-start path — fill the authoring template by hand. No content, no
scraping, no spend required. Onboarding is never blocked by a lack of history.

todo.name: brand_registered
todo.remediation: Register at least one brand: write brands/<id>/brand.json from the template (§2.4 step 1, DD-10).

actions.label: Register a brand
actions.help: Create brands/<id>/brand.json from the template: an id, a display name, account class, and one platform.

inputs.name: brand
inputs.label: A short id for this brand (e.g. my-brand)

actions.label: Where does this brand’s existing content come from?
actions.help: Pick how the engine learns the voice. (Phase 2 makes these one-click; today each option is a command.)

options.label: I have files / official exports — point to them
options.help: Import an export or a folder of posts, then build the DNA from it (free; no scraping).

options.label: Auto-fetch from my public account (Apify)
options.help: Bring-your-own scraper adapter pulls recent posts, then builds the DNA. Metered — shows a cost estimate first.
options.command: node bin/engine.js ingest-brand --brand <BRAND_ID>   # then confirm with --yes

options.label: Start cold — no content yet
options.help: Author the Brand DNA by hand from the template and set cold_start:true. Always works, zero spend.
options.command: cp templates/brand/brand-dna-authoring.md $CONTENT_HOME/brands/<BRAND_ID>/brand-dna.md   # then fill it in; set cold_start:true in brand.json

actions.label: Check this step
actions.help: Re-runs the checker for this step. When it passes, re-run `engine setup` to move on.
